_comment: Illustrative LLM output. Actual output will vary.

assessed_changes.title: EU AI Act — High-risk system classification guidelines
assessed_changes.required_action: Review all AI-powered features against high-risk classification criteria. Escalate to legal. Assess whether employment or credit decision features qualify.

assessed_changes.title: FTC rule on AI-generated endorsements
assessed_changes.required_action: Audit any feature that could generate customer-facing reviews or testimonials. Add disclosure labels if AI-generated content is present. Consult legal on enforcement timeline.

assessed_changes.title: UK ICO automated decision-making guidance
assessed_changes.required_action: Identify any decision-making flows that produce legally significant outcomes. Ensure human review option is documented. Update privacy notice if needed.

assessed_changes.title: California AB-2013 training data transparency
assessed_changes.required_action: Document training data sources for all AI features. Prepare public-facing summary. Effective January 2027.

assessed_changes.title: NIST AI RMF v2.0 — audit trails
assessed_changes.required_action: Monitor only. Non-binding, but may influence enterprise customer procurement questionnaires.

assessment.summary: Two high-priority items this week: the EU AI Act high-risk system guidelines and the FTC AI endorsement rule both directly affect Acme Analytics' AI output features and have near-term compliance deadlines. California AB-2013 adds a January 2027 training data disclosure requirement. Legal review recommended within 5 business days on the two high-priority items.
